skill.name: read-github
skill.description: Read and search public GitHub repository documentation and code through the gitmcp.io MCP service.
skill.summary: Access GitHub repository docs and code search through GitMCP.
skill.author: am-will

security_audit.summary: Static findings for subprocess and network behavior are largely confirmed in scripts/gitmcp.py: the skill launches npx mcp-remote and connects to MCP URLs derived from user input. The markdown backtick and weak cryptography findings are false positives from documentation text and argparse strings, but the remote tool execution and arbitrary URL handling make this high risk for a public marketplace.

high_findings.title: Remote MCP Subprocess Execution With User-Controlled Endpoint
high_findings.description: The script launches npx mcp-remote and passes an MCP URL derived from the repo argument. GitHub URLs are converted to gitmcp.io, but arbitrary non-GitHub HTTP URLs can pass through unchanged, allowing the process to connect to untrusted MCP endpoints.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess calls are direct and the endpoint is constructed from CLI input. The use of argument arrays reduces shell injection risk, but the remote endpoint trust issue is clearly present.

medium_findings.title: Unrestricted URL Fetch Through Remote Tool
medium_findings.description: The fetch-url command accepts a user-supplied URL and forwards it to the remote fetch_generic_url_content MCP tool. This is legitimate for documentation lookup, but it can retrieve arbitrary untrusted content into the workflow and may be abused if endpoint controls are absent.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URL parameter is explicitly accepted and forwarded to the MCP tool. The actual network fetch occurs in the remote service, so impact depends on that service controls.

medium_findings.title: Unpinned External npm Tooling
medium_findings.description: The script runs npx -y mcp-remote without pinning a package version. This creates supply-chain risk because future package changes can alter behavior without a local code change.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The npx invocation and missing version pin are visible in both subprocess calls. This is a common supply-chain concern, although not evidence of malicious intent.

low_findings.title: Markdown Command Examples Flagged as Shell Execution
low_findings.description: Static analysis flagged backtick-delimited command examples in SKILL.md. These are documentation examples, not runtime shell execution, although they do instruct users to run the Python script.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged content is inside markdown code fences and inline code. It does not execute by itself, so the scanner signal is a false positive for runtime execution.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Matches Are False Positives
low_findings.description: Static analysis reported weak cryptographic algorithm matches, but no evidence of cryptographic hashing or encryption calls was found in the reviewed files. The reported locations are descriptive text or parser configuration.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed code imports no crypto library and contains no hashing operation at the cited lines. The static finding appears to be a keyword collision.

dangerous_patterns.title: subprocess.Popen Invokes npx mcp-remote
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill uses Python subprocesses to run external npm tooling. The call uses an argument list instead of shell=True, but it still executes external code and starts a remote MCP session.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The exact subprocess pattern appears twice and directly starts mcp-remote. The risk is external process and remote tool execution, not shell metacharacter injection.

dangerous_patterns.title: Non-GitHub URLs Are Not Rejected
dangerous_patterns.description: The URL conversion helper returns inputs unchanged when they are not GitHub URLs or owner/repo paths. This allows arbitrary MCP URLs to reach mcp-remote.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The fallback return path and later use of mcp_url are visible in the code. Legitimate use is possible, but endpoint validation is absent.

content.value_statement: Repository documentation can be difficult to inspect quickly during coding work. This skill routes GitHub repositories through GitMCP so Claude, Codex, or Claude Code can fetch docs, search docs, and search code.
actual_capabilities: Converts GitHub repository URLs and owner/repo paths into gitmcp.io MCP endpoints.
Lists MCP tools exposed for a selected repository.
Fetches repository documentation through the GitMCP documentation tool.
Runs semantic documentation search for a user-supplied query.
Runs exact code search through the GitMCP code search tool.
Calls named MCP tools with user-provided JSON arguments.
limitations: Requires network access to gitmcp.io and npm package execution through npx.
Does not pin the mcp-remote package version used by npx.
Does not validate arbitrary non-GitHub MCP URLs supplied by the user.
Results depend on GitMCP service availability and repository indexing.

use_cases.title: Understand a New Repository
use_cases.description: Fetch documentation and summarize the purpose, setup steps, and main entry points for a public repository.

use_cases.title: Find Implementation Details
use_cases.description: Search repository code for a function, class, command, or configuration term without cloning the project.
use_cases.target_user: Maintainers and reviewers checking specific behavior

use_cases.title: Answer Documentation Questions
use_cases.description: Search repository docs for usage guidance, options, examples, or troubleshooting details.

prompt_templates.title: Summarize a Repository
prompt_templates.prompt: Read the documentation for github.com/owner/repo and summarize what the project does, how to install it, and the first command to try.

prompt_templates.title: Search Documentation
prompt_templates.prompt: Search the docs for owner/repo for information about authentication. Return the most relevant sections and explain the setup steps.

prompt_templates.title: Find Code Usage
prompt_templates.scenario: Code search for an implementation detail
prompt_templates.prompt: Search the code in owner/repo for the term cache invalidation. Explain where it appears and what each result likely controls.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Docs and Code
prompt_templates.prompt: Fetch the docs for owner/repo, then search code for the documented configuration names. Identify any documented options that are hard to find in code.

output_examples.input: Read github.com/example/project and explain the setup path.
output_examples.output: The response summarizes the project purpose, required dependencies, installation steps, and the first documented command to run.

output_examples.input: Search owner/repo docs for deployment options.
output_examples.output: The response lists the most relevant documentation sections, explains each deployment option, and notes where further reading is needed.

output_examples.input: Find code references for a specific function name.
output_examples.output: The response identifies matching files, describes likely roles for the matches, and recommends which files to inspect first.

best_practices: Use owner/repo inputs or normal GitHub URLs instead of arbitrary MCP URLs.
Review fetched content before relying on it for security-sensitive decisions.
Pin and review external npm tooling before using this skill in automated workflows.
anti_patterns: Do not run this skill on untrusted arbitrary MCP endpoints.
Do not pass secrets, private URLs, or credentials as tool arguments.
Do not treat fetched repository content as verified project behavior without checking source files.

faq.question: Does this skill clone repositories?
faq.answer: No. It accesses repository documentation and search results through the gitmcp.io MCP service.

faq.question: Can it read private repositories?
faq.answer: No evidence found that it supports private repository authentication. It is best suited for public repositories.

faq.question: Does it execute repository code?
faq.answer: No evidence found that it executes repository code. It does execute npx mcp-remote to reach an MCP server.

faq.question: Why does it need network access?
faq.answer: It connects to gitmcp.io and may fetch URLs through the GitMCP service when requested.

faq.question: Can it search both docs and code?
faq.answer: Yes. The script exposes separate commands for documentation search and exact code search.

faq.question: What should users verify before publishing it internally?
faq.answer: Users should review the remote MCP trust model, restrict allowed endpoints, and pin external package versions.
